steps.title: Compare the authors' claims
steps.content: Text 1 claims digital art is not really art because technology does most of the work. Text 2 pushes against that view by saying digital artists need art fundamentals, sophisticated software knowledge, and often begin with drawings on paper.

steps.title: Avoid overclaims
steps.content: Choice A is about displaying art, not making it. Choice B says digital art is harder than traditional painting, which Text 2 never claims. Choice D contradicts Text 2's point that fundamentals matter. Text 2 would instead stress real skill with nontraditional tools. The answer is C.
